summary: The diff implements all four requirements. REQ-1 (Refresh button force-refetch) is addressed in AdminPage.tsx with invalidateQueries + removeQueries + refetch and staleTime:0/gcTime:0 in useQueries.ts. REQ-2 (active count sync) is addressed via live-computed stats from fetched appList. REQ-4 (admin credentials) is preserved in useAdminAuth.ts with both credential pairs and 24-hour localStorage session. REQ-3 (Set Price / Unauthorized fix) is partially addressed with token validation in AdminPage.tsx, but the diff does not clearly show how the admin actor is authenticated at the backend actor level in useQueries.ts to prevent the Unauthorized error.

missing_requirements.requirement: The admin actor used for the setFee call must carry a valid identity/session so that the backend does not return Unauthorized. After a successful Set Price call, the updated fee must be immediately visible to customers on the DashboardPage without requiring a page reload.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows token validation and error display in AdminPage.tsx, but the truncated useQueries.ts does not show how useSetApplicationFee uses an admin-authenticated actor. The backend main.mo still checks AccessControl.hasPermission for #admin. There is no clear evidence that the actor used in the setFee mutation is admin-authenticated at the ICP identity level. DashboardPage fee visibility after Set Price via query invalidation is also not confirmed in the truncated diff.
